question.text: The minimum qualification requirement for an individual retirement adviser includes:

answer.text: Post-graduation in finance with 5 years of experience
Graduation in any discipline with NISM Series XVII certification
MBA in finance from a recognized university
Only NISM Series XVII certification without any graduation requirement

explanation.text: **The Logic:** The regulations require: (1) graduation in any discipline OR professional qualification with 3 years post-qualification experience, AND (2) NISM Series XVII certification (or equivalent approved by PFRDA). **The Trap:** Option D is incorrect — graduation is required in addition to NISM certification. Option A overstates the requirement. Option C specifies MBA which is not mandatory. **Cross-Exam Context:** Qualification requirements differ across intermediary types: investment advisers (NISM X-A/X-B), MF distributors (NISM V-A), retirement advisers (NISM XVII). **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** NISM XVII certification must be valid at all times. Plan for renewal through continuing professional education before expiry to avoid interruption in practice.
